title: Intraday Ledger Reconciliation Loop
description: A reconciliation and back-office workflow that resolves breaks, validates accounting alignment, and closes audit-critical exceptions. Focus case: intraday ledger reconciliation loop.
assumptions: Ledger feeds and external source files are timestamped and arrive within the intraday processing window.
Matching keys and account mappings are deterministic for a high proportion of routine cases; residuals require manual investigation.
Validation profiles encapsulate all applicable accounting rules and tolerance thresholds for intraday checks.
Operators have access to required supporting documents and counterparty confirmations via the document_reference pointers.
risks: Data latency or missing source files can prevent automated matching and force prolonged manual investigation.
Incorrect account mappings or validation profile misconfiguration may produce false positives or false negatives in reconciliation.
Manual overrides or ad‑hoc adjustments without adequate audit entries may breach regulatory traceability requirements.
Operator assignment bottlenecks or insufficient staffing during peak intraday windows can delay closure and increase settlement risk.
Escalation matrix misrouting may expose cases to inappropriate approvers, causing rework and audit contention.
Control identifiers not propagated across systems can prevent reliable end‑to‑end traceability for audit and regulatory reporting.
